Home›Services› Threat Intelligence
Service

Threat Intelligence
& Hunting

Understanding who is targeting your organization is as critical as maintaining defenses. Our practice delivers adversary-focused intelligence and proactive threat hunting — grounded in operational experience tracking nation-state and criminal threat groups.

Intelligence That Drives Action

The majority of threat intelligence available today is noise — vendor feeds populated with indicators of compromise that were stale days ago. Our approach is fundamentally different. We focus on the adversaries actively targeting your industry, understand their tactics and procedures, and translate that intelligence into specific defensive actions your team can implement immediately.

This is the work our practice is known for. Our founder’s 26,000+ professional following was built on deeply technical threat intelligence analysis that practitioners trust and apply. No hype, no exaggeration — actionable intelligence delivered with rigor.

Capabilities

  • Adversary profiling and threat landscape assessments
  • Proactive threat hunting across endpoint, network, and cloud environments
  • APT group tracking and analysis (nation-state and criminal)
  • Indicator of compromise (IOC) analysis and enrichment
  • MITRE ATT&CK-based threat modeling
  • Threat intelligence program design and maturation
  • Dark web and underground forum monitoring
  • Executive threat briefings and board-level reporting
  • Intelligence integration with SOC detection workflows

Who We Serve

Security teams seeking to transition from reactive to proactive operations. Organizations in regulated industries — defense, healthcare, financial services — that face targeted threats. CISOs who need to articulate risk to the board in concrete, actionable terms. Organizations seeking intelligence capabilities that change how they operate, not just what they report.

Capabilities

  • APT Tracking
  • Threat Hunting
  • IOC Analysis
  • MITRE ATT&CK
  • Dark Web Monitoring
  • Executive Briefings
  • Intel Program Design

Built on Credibility

26,000+ cybersecurity professionals follow this work. Operational, not theoretical.
